title: REGRESSION: All audit tools broken — extension failed to activate
description: Root cause: 'import MarkdownIt from markdown-it' was added to doc-preview.ts as a Node-side import. The extension uses 'vsce package --no-dependencies' so markdown-it was NOT included in the VSIX. On load, Node's require() threw MODULE_NOT_FOUND, crashing the entire extension host. ALL registered commands became unavailable — every audit tool, launcher, doc catalog, npm scripts, everything. The import was introduced when attempting to upgrade mdToHtml() from a regex chain to a real parser. Fix: removed the Node-side import entirely. markdown-it is now loaded from cdnjs in the webview HTML (same CDN pattern as highlight.js). Rendering happens client-side via window.markdownit(). Package.json dependencies block cleaned up.
howItHappened: During markdown-it install, 'npm install @types/markdown-it --save-dev' ran in the wrong directory (DiskCleanUp instead of cielovista-tools), wiping 303 packages. Recovery 'npm install markdown-it @types/markdown-it' only restored 6 packages and added markdown-it to 'dependencies' instead of devDependencies. The import was then added to doc-preview.ts without realising --no-dependencies packaging would exclude it.
whenItHappened: 2026-03-18, this session, after the markdown-it integration attempt
filesChanged: src/shared/doc-preview.ts — removed import, switched to CDN client-side render
package.json — removed markdown-it and @types/markdown-it from dependencies

title: FEATURE: MCP Endpoint Viewer — live in-browser viewer for the 4 catalog MCP endpoints
description: Added a new CVT feature that spins up a local HTTP server (auto-assigned port, same pattern as View a Doc) and opens it in the system browser. The viewer has 4 tabs — one per MCP catalog endpoint: list_projects, find_project, search_docs, get_catalog. Each tab calls a matching /api/* route that reuses loadRegistry() from src/shared/registry and buildCatalog() from doc-catalog/commands — zero logic duplication, so the HTTP endpoints return the same data shape the MCP tools return. Dark theme matching View a Doc, yellow (#FFD700) links per project link-visibility rule. New command cvs.mcp.viewer.open registered, wired into Quick Launch as the 7th button (🔌 MCP Viewer), added to home-page OPEN_DIRECT list so it bypasses the streaming output wrapper, and registered at Dewey 700.005 with scope=global, action=read. New feature toggle cielovistaTools.features.mcpViewer (default true). Rebuild green: 13/13 catalog integrity, 16/16 regression, 47/47 install verify.
filesChanged: src/features/mcp-viewer/index.ts — NEW: HTTP server + activate/deactivate + 4 endpoint handlers
src/features/mcp-viewer/html.ts — NEW: browser UI with 4 tabs, controls per endpoint, grouped table rendering
src/extension.ts — import mcpViewerActivate/Deactivate, activateIfEnabled('mcpViewer'), add to deactivate chain
src/features/home-page.ts — Quick Launch 🔌 MCP Viewer button, added cmd to OPEN_DIRECT list
src/features/cvs-command-launcher/catalog.ts — Dewey 700.005 entry for cvs.mcp.viewer.open
package.json — command contribution + cielovistaTools.features.mcpViewer toggle

title: FEATURE: Symbol index MCP tools and viewer tabs — reusable-code discovery across 18 projects
description: Added three new MCP tools and matching viewer tabs that give Claude and the user compiler-style symbol discovery across every registered project — closes the 'does this helper already exist' gap that was causing reuse misses. list_symbols scans .d.ts from each project's out/ plus raw .ts/.js from src, scripts, and tests, returning every exported or top-level symbol with name, kind, signature, JSDoc, source file, line number, and role (src|script|test|declaration). find_symbol does exact-then-prefix lookup by name — the MCP equivalent of IDE Go-to-Definition. list_cvt_commands returns the 83-entry CVT command catalog as structured JSON so existing commands can be found without re-reading catalog.ts. Viewer gained three tabs matching the three tools; same dark theme and yellow links as the rest. Implementation: new mcp-server/src/symbol-index.ts plus extension-side port at src/features/mcp-viewer/symbol-index.ts — the two live in different Node module systems so each has its own compiled artifact, kept in sync by hand with the MCP server as source of truth. Cache is lazy and invalidated on folder mtime change. Zero dependencies — pure regex over predictable tsc-generated .d.ts output for the TypeScript side and top-level declarations for the JavaScript side. Also fixed three pre-existing issues exposed by the build chain: REG-002 logError violations in explorer-copy-path-to-chat.ts and mcp-server-status.ts (pattern required inline err-instanceof-Error rather than a captured variable); REG-010 updated to recognize mcp-server/src/ imports as satisfying the dependency-usage rule (the MCP SDK is genuinely used in mcp-server/src/ but never in src/); install-verify VSIX size ceiling raised from 5 MB to 10 MB to accommodate the MCP SDK weight; install-verify extension-listed check switched from running `code-insiders --list-extensions` (unreliable while Insiders is running) to checking the installed extension folder on disk (what actually loads at reload).
filesChanged: mcp-server/src/symbol-index.ts — NEW: registry-aware symbol scanner, d.ts + .js parsers, mtime cache, filter + find helpers, CVT command catalog parser
mcp-server/src/tools/definitions.ts — 3 new zod schemas: ListSymbolsToolSchema, FindSymbolToolSchema, ListCvtCommandsToolSchema
mcp-server/src/tools/index.ts — 3 new server.tool() registrations wired to symbol-index helpers
src/features/mcp-viewer/symbol-index.ts — NEW: extension-side port of the symbol index (CommonJS)
src/features/mcp-viewer/index.ts — 3 new /api routes: /api/list_symbols, /api/find_symbol, /api/list_cvt_commands
src/features/mcp-viewer/html.ts — 3 new tabs, control templates, renderers (renderSymbolsTable, renderCvtCommandsTable), updated dispatcher and countSummary
src/features/mcp-server-status.ts — REG-002 fix: logError calls now pass '' as arg2 with full context in arg1
src/features/explorer-copy-path-to-chat.ts — REG-002 fix: inline err-instanceof-Error in arg2
scripts/run-regression-tests.js — REG-010 now scans mcp-server/src/ in addition to src/
tests/install-verify.test.js — size ceiling 5MB → 10MB, listed-check now reads installed folder on disk

title: BUG: MCP Viewer crashed with regex SyntaxError — single-backslash regex literals in template literal silently stripped
description: Root cause: buildViewerHtml in src/features/mcp-viewer/html.ts returns a TypeScript template literal containing browser JavaScript. The renderSymbolsTable function stripped JSDoc markers with three inline regex literals: /^\s*\/\*\*/, /\*\/\s*$/, and /^\s*\*\s?/gm. Inside the outer template literal the sequences \s, \/, and \* are not recognized string escapes, so JavaScript silently drops the backslash at string-build time. The HTML sent to the browser therefore contained /^s*/**/, /*/s*$/, and /^s*s?/gm. The browser's JS parser read /^s*/ as a valid regex, hit ** as exponentiation, then started a second regex at the next /, whose body became the literal text ', '').replace(' — which contained an unmatched ) and threw Invalid regular expression: /, '').replace(/: Unmatched ')'. Fix: doubled the backslashes in the source so the emitted HTML contains the single-backslash regex literals the browser needs. Same pattern the adjacent split(/[\\/]/) line was already using to produce /[\\/]/ in the browser. Verified by a new test script scripts/verify-mcp-viewer-fix.js that calls buildViewerHtml, extracts the <script> block, and runs new Function(body) — same parser the browser uses. The script now parses; before the fix it threw the exact SyntaxError. Missed during FIX-003 because the parking lot assumed the root cause was */ terminating an HTML comment; actual cause was plain string-literal escape handling, and testing stopped at the MCP tool call layer instead of loading the HTML. Lesson: server-side MCP verification does not cover HTML-template output; always execute buildViewerHtml and parse its <script> body.
filesChanged: src/features/mcp-viewer/html.ts — doubled backslashes on the three JSDoc-stripping regex literals inside renderSymbolsTable
scripts/verify-mcp-viewer-fix.js — NEW: executes buildViewerHtml, asserts emitted <script> body parses via new Function()
